2606.21638.title: Toward Open Weight Models Without Risks: Separating Public and Private Capabilities in LLMs
2606.21638.paper_summary: Open-weight Large Language Models (LLMs) enable scientific progress and broad deployment. However, they make it difficult to control access to sensitive capabilities. Current practice either suppresses dangerous capabilities before release or mediates access through closed services that use specialized model variants, input/output monitors, and API permissions. The former is susceptible to jailbreaks while sacrificing capability for all users to mitigate the risks posed by a few, and the latter is fundamentally incompatible with open-weight release. In this paper, we propose Tiered Language Models (TLMs), where a single set of released weights supports multiple capability levels. In its default public configuration, a TLM behaves as a conventional LLM. A compact secret key specifies a permutation over a small parameter subset, inducing an alternative computation graph over the same weights that exposes additional capabilities. We develop a training protocol that jointly pretrains both configurations from scratch, then fine-tunes the keyed configuration on private data with regularization to preserve the public model's behavior. We pretrain 180M- and 650M-parameter TLMs and demonstrate that the keyed configuration can acquire a new language, gain instruction-following ability, and memorize private factual knowledge, whereas the public configuration exhibits none of these capabilities. Moreover, we show that our approach extends naturally to multiple hierarchical tiers. Because authorization operates on the model's weight structure rather than in the input space, the mechanism resists fine-tuning-based extraction and partial key compromise. In general, TLMs take a step toward reconciling open-weight release with selective capability control.
2606.21638.paper_summary_zh: 開放權重的大型語言模型（LLMs）促進了科學進步和廣泛部署。<lb/>然而，它們使得控制對敏感能力的訪問變得困難。<lb/>當前的做法要麼在發布前抑制危險能力，要麼通過使用專門模型變體、輸入/輸出監控和API權限的封閉服務來調解訪問。<lb/>前者容易受到越獄攻擊，同時犧牲所有用戶的能力以減輕少數人帶來的風險，而後者與開放權重的發布根本不兼容。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了分層語言模型（TLMs），其中一組發布的權重支持多個能力級別。<lb/>在其默認公共配置中，TLM的行為類似於傳統的LLM。<lb/>一個緊湊的秘密密鑰指定了一小部分參數的排列，從而在相同的權重上引入了一個替代計算圖，揭示了額外的能力。<lb/>我們開發了一個訓練協議，從零開始共同預訓練這兩種配置，然後在私有數據上對帶密鑰的配置進行微調，並使用正則化來保留公共模型的行為。<lb/>我們預訓練了180M和650M參數的TLMs，並展示了帶密鑰的配置可以獲得一種新語言、獲得指令跟隨能力並記住私有事實知識，而公共配置則不具備這些能力。<lb/>此外，我們還展示了我們的方法自然地擴展到多個層級。<lb/>因為授權是在模型的權重結構上運作，而不是在輸入空間中，這一機制抵抗基於微調的提取和部分密鑰妥協。<lb/>總的來說，TLMs朝著將開放權重發布與選擇性能力控制調和邁出了一步。
2606.21638.author: Charbel El Feghali et.al.
2606.21638.authors: Charbel El Feghali, Arkil Patel, Nicholas Meade, Spandana Gella, Verna Dankers, Siva Reddy
